volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 4.2.857b - Section 4.2.896)

sections.label: Section 4.2.857b
sections.original: At tu appone et respice ad me .
sections.translation: Then , do you set it down , and turn your face to me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.857c
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so , although I have no leisure .

sections.label: Section 4.2.858b
sections.translation: Save you , Syncerastus .

sections.label: Section 4.2.858c
sections.translation: O Milphio , may all the Gods and Goddesses favour —

sections.label: Section 4.2.859b
sections.translation: What person , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.859c
sections.translation: Neither you , nor me , Milphio , nor my own master , in fact .

sections.label: Section 4.2.860b
sections.translation: Whom are they to favour , then ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.860c
sections.translation: Any one else they please ; for not one of us is deserving of it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.861b
sections.translation: You speak wittily .

sections.label: Section 4.2.861c
sections.translation: It befits me to do so .

sections.label: Section 4.2.862
sections.translation: What are you doing ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.862b
sections.original: Facio quod manufesti moechi haú ferme solent .
sections.translation: I ’ m doing that which , clearly , adulterers don ’ t generally do .

sections.label: Section 4.2.863
sections.translation: What ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.863b
sections.translation: Bringing all off in safety [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.863c
sections.original: Di te et tuom erum perduint .
sections.translation: May the Gods confound you and your master !

sections.label: Section 4.2.864
sections.original: Me non perdent ; illum ut perdant facere possum , si velim ,
sections.translation: May they not confound me . I could make them ruin him , if I chose —

sections.label: Section 4.2.865
sections.original: meum erum ut perdant , ni mihi metuam , Milphio .
sections.translation: ruin my master , did I not fear for myself , Milphio .

sections.label: Section 4.2.865b
sections.translation: What is it ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.866
sections.translation: You are a bad one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.866b
sections.translation: I am a bad one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.866c
sections.translation: It goes but badly with me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.866d
sections.translation: Just tell me , then ; you ought to be in quite other plight . Why is it that it goes badly with you , who have at home in superabundance what to eat , and what to drink ? You don ’ t give a single three-obol piece away to a mistress , and have her for nothing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.869
sections.translation: May Jupiter so love me —

sections.label: Section 4.2.869b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , in the degree that you deserve , to wit .

sections.label: Section 4.2.870
sections.original: Vt ego hanc familiam interire cupio .
sections.translation: How I do long for this family to come to ruin .

sections.label: Section 4.2.870b
sections.translation: If you long for it , lend your aid .

sections.label: Section 4.2.871
sections.original: Sine pennis volare hau facilest : meae alae pennas non habent .
sections.translation: Without feathers it isn ’ t easy to fly : my wings have got no feathers .

sections.label: Section 4.2.872
sections.original: Nolito edepol devellisse : iam his duobus mensibus
sections.translation: Troth , then , don ’ t pluck out any hairs ; then , in the next two months , your arm-pits will be fit for flying .

sections.label: Section 4.2.873b
sections.translation: Away to utter perdition !

sections.label: Section 4.2.873c
sections.translation: Away yourself , and your master !

sections.label: Section 4.2.874
sections.original: Verum . enim qui hómo eum norit , norit . cito homo pervorti potest .
sections.translation: But , really , if a person knew him well , the fellow might soon be ruined .

sections.label: Section 4.2.875
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.875b
sections.original: Quasi tu tacitum habere quicquam potis sis .
sections.translation: ⋯ Just as though you could be silent on any matter .

sections.label: Section 4.2.875c
sections.translation: I ’ ll keep the matter more strictly secret for you than that which has been told to a dumb woman .

sections.label: Section 4.2.877
sections.original: Animum inducam facile ut tibi istuc credam , ni te noverim .
sections.translation: I could easily bring my mind to believe you there , if I did not know you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.878
sections.translation: Trust me boldly at my own peril .

sections.label: Section 4.2.878b
sections.translation: I shall trust you to my cost , and still I will trust you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.879
sections.original: Scin tu erum tuom meo ero esse inimicum capitalem ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know that your master is a mortal enemy of my master ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.879b
sections.translation: I know it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.880
sections.translation: By reason of the love affair ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.880b
sections.translation: You are losing all your pains .

sections.label: Section 4.2.880c
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.880d
sections.translation: Because you are teaching one that has been taught .

sections.label: Section 4.2.881
sections.original: Quid ergo dubitas quin lubenter tuo ero meus quid possiet
sections.translation: Why , then , do you doubt that my master will do a mischief to your master with pleasure , so far as he can do , with his deserving it ? Then besides , if you lend some assistance , on that account he ’ ll be able to do it the more easily .

sections.label: Section 4.2.883b
sections.translation: But I ’ m afraid of this , Milphio —

sections.label: Section 4.2.884
sections.translation: What is it that you ’ re afraid of ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.884b
sections.original: Dúm ero insidias paritem , ne me perduim .
sections.translation: That while I ’ m preparing the plot against my master , I may be betrayed by yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.885
sections.original: si erus meus me esse elocutum quoiquam mortali sciat ,
sections.translation: If my master knows that I ’ ve been talking to any individual , he ’ ll forthwith be making me from Syncerastus into Brokenlegs [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.887
sections.original: Numquam edepol mortalis quisquam fiet e me certior ,
sections.translation: On my word , never shall any mortal be made the wiser by me ; only to my master alone will I tell it ; and to him , too , in such a way that he shall not disclose that this matter originated in yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.889b
sections.translation: I shall trust you at my peril , and yet I will trust you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.890
sections.original: sed hoc tu tecum tacitum habeto .
sections.translation: But do you keep this a secret to yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.890b
sections.translation: To Faith herself it is not more safely confided . Speak out boldly ( there ’ s room and opportunity ); we are here alone .

sections.label: Section 4.2.892
sections.original: Erus si tuos volt facere frugem , meum erum perdet .
sections.translation: If your master chooses to act with caution , he ’ ll prove the ruin of my master .

sections.label: Section 4.2.892b
sections.translation: How can that be ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.893
sections.translation: Easily .

sections.label: Section 4.2.893b
sections.original: Fac ergo id facile noscam ego , ut ille possit noscere .
sections.translation: Then let me be acquainted with this easily , that he may know it as well .

sections.label: Section 4.2.894
sections.original: Quia Adelphasium , quám erus deamat tuos , ingenuast .
sections.translation: Because Adelphasium , whom your master dotes on is free by birth .

sections.label: Section 4.2.894b
sections.translation: In what way ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.895
sections.original: Eodem quo soror illius altera Anterastilis .
sections.translation: In the same way that her other sister Anterastylis is .

sections.label: Section 4.2.896
sections.translation: But how am I to believe that ?
